sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"Current state:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'M', 'O'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Step 1: Click dark gray at top-right to swap
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 9, 'col': 49}])
print(f"\nAfter swap: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# Check state after swap
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"After swap:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'M', 'O'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Step 2: Advance white by clicking magenta
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    # Find magenta on the trail
    magenta_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']
    print(f"\nMagenta nodes: {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in magenta_nodes]}")
    
    # Click the first magenta (should be on the trail from white to dark gray)
    for i in range(5):
        magenta = None
        for n in nodes:
            if n['color'] == 'M':
                magenta = n['boundary'][0]
                break
        if not magenta:
            print(f"No magenta found")
            break
        result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': magenta[0], 'col': magenta[1]}])
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
            print(f"Level completed at advance {i+1}!")
            break
        seg = current_frame.segmentation
        nodes = seg['nodes']
        # Check white and dark gray positions
        for n in nodes:
            if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 12:
                print(f"After advance {i+1}: white={n['boundary']}")
            if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12:
                print(f"After advance {i+1}: dark_gray={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"Current state:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'M', 'O'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Step 1: Click dark gray at top-right to swap
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 9, 'col': 49}])
print(f"\nAfter swap: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# Check state after swap
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"After swap:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'M', 'O'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Step 2: Advance white by clicking magenta
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    # Find magenta on the trail
    magenta_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']
    print(f"\nMagenta nodes: {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in magenta_nodes]}")
    
    # Click the first magenta (should be on the trail from white to dark gray)
    for i in range(5):
        magenta = None
        for n in nodes:
            if n['color'] == 'M':
                magenta = n['boundary'][0]
                break
        if not magenta:
            print(f"No magenta found")
            break
        result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': magenta[0], 'col': magenta[1]}])
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
            print(f"Level completed at advance {i+1}!")
            break
        seg = current_frame.segmentation
        nodes = seg['nodes']
        # Check white and dark gray positions
        for n in nodes:
            if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 12:
                print(f"After advance {i+1}: white={n['boundary']}")
            if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12:
                print(f"After advance {i+1}: dark_gray={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
